name: Meilisearch
what: Fast, developer-friendly full-text search engine for application search and filtering.
solves: Site search, knowledge search, product search, and internal-search UX with typo tolerance and ranking controls.
users: Web and product teams needing search without Elasticsearch-level complexity.
fit: Good fit if Hylono needs strong search across content, products, protocols, or docs.
runtime: Rust service with index storage, REST API, and API keys.
components: Search engine, indexes, API keys, optional sync/indexing pipelines, and front-end clients.
deploy: Operationally light compared with Elasticsearch-class systems.
deps: Persistent storage, indexing pipeline, API key governance, and monitoring.
auth: Master/search/private API keys and tenant tokens.
security: Protect master keys and avoid indexing sensitive internal data into public search endpoints.
setup_path: Good later addition if current site search becomes a real product need.
env: Master key, storage path, env mode, dump/snapshot config, and network settings.
persistence: Use dumps/snapshots and backups of index data or rebuild pipelines.
operations: Reindexing strategy, ranking tuning, and index freshness matter.
failure_points: Indexing the wrong data, stale indexes, or exposing privileged search datasets.
integration: Strong fit for Next.js content/product search and possibly BookStack/Docusaurus search.
verify: Clarify which searchable surfaces matter first: site content, products, docs, or internal ops.
gdpr: Search indexes can duplicate personal data if not curated.
recommendation: LATER / HIGH-UTILITY IF SEARCH BECOMES IMPORTANT
codex_read_first: Read actual search requirements and content architecture before implementing.
codex_best_path: Add Meilisearch when there is a clear search surface worth indexing.
codex_never_assume: Never index sensitive datasets into a public-facing search service.
